meaning: adj.瞎的,盲目的vt.使失明,缺乏眼光或判断力
examples: Mark has been blind from birth.
Doctors think he will go blind.
He was a teacher of the blind.
There were, in other words, many reasons why mutual-fund executives turned a blind eye to such practices.
At the centre of the group was a blind boy, as alert and as slight as a gazelle.
